title: Restaurant robot guides
metaDescription: Restaurant robot guides: serving and bussing robot costs, layout fit, and when front-of-house automation makes sense for QSR, buffet, and full-service dining.
intro: Restaurant robots focus on food running, bussing, and guest-facing tasks — not full server replacement. These guides help operators budget and assess layout fit before leasing or buying.

summarySections.heading: What this topic covers
bullets: Serving and bussing robot cost and lease ranges
Peak-hour utilization vs headline monthly fees
Links to runner comparison and matcher

faqs.question: How much does a restaurant serving robot cost?
faqs.answer: Purchase prices often fall in the mid five figures per unit; lease and RaaS programs commonly run roughly $500–$1,500/month per robot depending on model and fleet size.

faqs.question: Do serving robots work in tight dining rooms?
faqs.answer: Open layouts and wide aisles work best. Fine-dining rooms with narrow paths often see lower fit scores — measure aisles before shortlisting vendors.
